compiled_state.title: As a project maintainer, I can rely on platform compatibility being validated as a required PR check
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Promote `ticket-platform-matrix.yml` from a path-filtered workflow to an always-firing required PR check by removing or broadening the existing path filter on its pull_request trigger.
**Why**: Closes G8-M3 from the 2026-04-26 retrospective. Platform compatibility (Linux bash 4, macOS bash 3.2, Alpine BusyBox) becomes a pre-merge gate so platform regressions cannot ship — addressing the tolerated-red pattern called out in process-gaps-4-26.md.
**Scope**:
- IN: Edit `ticket-platform-matrix.yml`'s pull_request trigger so it fires on any change under `plugins/dso/scripts/` or `plugins/dso/hooks/` (current path filter is too narrow). Verify all three matrix legs (`linux-bash4`, `macos-bash3`, `alpine-busybox`) appear as PR status checks on a no-op PR. Coordinate check-context naming with the Ruleset story (`fc8b-aab0`) so each leg's check name matches the Ruleset's required-check list.
- OUT: Fixing the underlying matrix-leg test failures (G8-M1 covers that under separate P1 bug tickets). Adding the matrix legs to the Ruleset's required-check list — that is part of `fc8b-aab0`'s expanded done definition; this story produces the check contexts. — Verified: epic description's Re-decomposition Plan section assigns Ruleset list expansion to `fc8b-aab0`.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, every PR opened against main triggers `ticket-platform-matrix.yml` regardless of which `plugins/dso/` paths it touches, and the three legs (`linux-bash4`, `macos-bash3`, `alpine-busybox`) appear as PR status checks
  ← Satisfies: "ticket-platform-matrix.yml fires on every PR (path filter removed or broadened to any change under plugins/dso/scripts/ or plugins/dso/hooks/); its three matrix legs (linux-bash4, macos-bash3, alpine-busybox) are each individually included in the Ruleset's required-check list"
- When this story is complete, the leg names exposed as PR check contexts match the names recorded in the Ruleset's required-check list (coordination with `fc8b-aab0`)
  ← Satisfies: "ticket-platform-matrix.yml fires on every PR ... three matrix legs ... included in the Ruleset's required-check list"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic (workflow file structural test verifying the pull_request trigger has no narrow path filter)
  ← Satisfies: TDD Done-of-Done

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Existing matrix legs may currently be red (G8 cited 7/7 failures); promoting them to required without first fixing them blocks all PRs. Sequence with G8-M1 P1 bug tickets so failing legs are fixed before the path filter is broadened, OR ship behind a temporary `continue-on-error: true` while fixing the legs (decision deferred to `/dso:implementation-plan`).
- [Maintainability] Check-context naming must match between this workflow file and the Ruleset payload — coordinate via shared constant or documented naming convention.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Consideration (adversarial review): [Consumer Impact] GitHub Actions `paths`/`paths-ignore` filters apply per top-level trigger only when declared at the workflow level. Removing/broadening the filter must be scoped to the pull_request trigger specifically; preserve the existing push and schedule trigger filters to avoid running 3 OS matrix legs on every main-branch commit. Document expected per-PR minute consumption increase.
comments.author: Test
